acme.title: ACME Protocol
acme.noAccountsDesc: Create your first ACME account to get started
acme.noCertificatesDesc: Certificates issued via ACME will appear here
acme.enableAcmeServerDesc: Allow automated certificate issuance via your own ACME server
acme.enableLetsEncryptProxyDesc: Forward requests to Let's Encrypt with automatic DNS-01 challenge handling
acme.proxyUsageNote: UCM automatically creates and cleans up DNS TXT records - no certbot DNS plugin needed!
acme.aboutAcmeDesc: ACME enables automated certificate issuance. Configure the server below, then use the directory URL with any ACME client (certbot, acme.sh, etc.).
acme.aboutAcmeInfo: ACME (Automatic Certificate Management Environment) enables automated certificate issuance. Compatible with certbot, acme.sh, and other ACME clients.
acme.letsEncryptProxyTip: Enable the LE Proxy to forward requests to Let's Encrypt while maintaining centralized audit logging and control.
acme.accountSecurityWarning: ACME accounts contain private keys. Deactivate or delete unused accounts to minimize security exposure.
acme.agreeToTerms: I agree to the ACME Terms of Service
acme.agreeToTermsRequired: You must agree to the Terms of Service
acme.letsEncryptAboutDesc: Request free SSL/TLS certificates from Let's Encrypt. Certificates are valid for 90 days and can be automatically renewed.
acme.viewHistoryForCertificates: Issued certificates are displayed in the History tab.
acme.noClientOrdersDesc: Request your first Let's Encrypt certificate to get started
acme.deleteOrderConfirmDesc: Are you sure you want to delete the order for {{domain}}? This action cannot be undone.
acme.environmentHelper: Use staging for testing to avoid rate limits
acme.autoRenewalDesc: Automatically renew certificates 30 days before expiration
acme.revokeOnRenewalDesc: Automatically revoke the previous certificate with reason 'superseded' when a new one is issued
acme.revokeExistingSupersededDesc: Revoke old certificates that have already been replaced by renewals
acme.revokeSupersededConfirmDesc: This will immediately revoke {{count}} certificate(s) that have been replaced by newer versions. This action is irreversible. Revoked certificates will appear in CRLs and be reported as revoked via OCSP.
acme.domainsHelper: One domain per line. Use *.domain.com for wildcards.
acme.atLeastOneDomainRequired: At least one domain is required
acme.challengeTypeHelper: DNS-01 is required for wildcards and doesn't need port 80 access
acme.dnsProviderHelper: Select a provider for automatic DNS record creation
acme.productionWarningDesc: Production certificates are rate limited. Use staging for testing.
acme.dnsProvidersAboutDesc: Configure DNS providers for automatic DNS-01 challenge validation. UCM will create and delete TXT records automatically.
acme.noDnsProvidersDesc: Add a DNS provider to enable automatic DNS-01 challenge validation
acme.manualDnsInfo: Manual mode requires you to create DNS TXT records manually when requesting certificates.
acme.domainsHelpDesc: Register domains and assign them to DNS providers. Only registered domains can request certificates via ACME Proxy.
acme.noDomainsDesc: Register your domains to enable ACME Proxy. Certbot and other ACME clients will be able to request certificates for these domains.
acme.domainNameHelper: Enter the root domain (e.g., example.com). Subdomains will inherit this configuration.
acme.localDomainHelper: Enter the domain this CA should issue certificates for. Subdomains will be matched automatically.
acme.noLocalDomainsDesc: Add a domain-to-CA mapping so the local ACME server knows which CA signs certificates for each domain.
acme.localDomainsHelp: Map domains to specific CAs. The local ACME server uses these mappings to sign certificates with the correct CA for each domain.
acme.autoApproveDesc: Automatically approve requests without manual review
acme.confirmDeleteDomain: Remove domain "{{domain}}"? ACME Proxy will no longer accept certificate requests for this domain.
acme.subtitle: {{count}} ACME accounts
acme.challengeValidated: Challenge validated — order ready for finalization
acme.orderStillProcessing: Order is still being validated by the ACME server

scep.title: SCEP Protocol
scep.noRequestsDesc: Enrollment requests will appear here when devices request certificates via SCEP protocol
scep.selectRequest: Select a request to view details
scep.enableScepDesc: Allow devices to enroll certificates via SCEP protocol
scep.useThisUrl: Use this URL in client configuration
scep.caIdentifierHelp: Identifier sent to clients during GetCACaps
scep.autoApproveWarning: Auto-approve enabled. Ensure challenge passwords are securely distributed.
scep.scepDisabledDesc: Enable SCEP in Configuration tab to use challenge passwords.
scep.noCasAvailableDesc: Create a Certificate Authority first to configure SCEP challenge passwords
scep.aboutScepDesc: Simple Certificate Enrollment Protocol (SCEP) enables automated certificate enrollment for devices like MDM-managed devices, network equipment, and IoT devices.
scep.mdmIntegrationDesc: Compatible with Microsoft Intune, Jamf Pro, VMware Workspace ONE, and other MDM solutions. Configure the SCEP URL and challenge password in your MDM profile.
scep.challengeSecurityDesc: Challenge passwords should be securely distributed. Consider using auto-approve only in trusted environments with proper network segmentation.

crlOcsp.subtitle: {{count}} active CRLs
crlOcsp.selectCA: Select a CA to manage CRLs
crlOcsp.createCAFirst: Create a CA first to manage CRLs
crlOcsp.crlDescription: Certificate Revocation Lists (CRLs) contain serial numbers of revoked certificates. They are periodically published and cached by clients for offline verification.
crlOcsp.ocspVsCRLDescription: OCSP provides real-time revocation status checks. CRL is a periodic snapshot. Enable both for maximum compatibility with all clients and applications.
crlOcsp.cdpNoteDescription: Include CDP (CRL Distribution Point) and AIA (Authority Information Access) URLs in your CA settings to enable automatic revocation checking.
crlOcsp.includeURLsNote: Include these URLs in CA settings for automatic revocation checking.

trustStore.bestPracticesDesc: Only add certificates from trusted sources.
trustStore.securityNoteDesc: Be careful when adding third-party certificates.

trustStore.syncConfirm: This will import up to 50 certificates from the system. Continue?
trustStore.subtitle: Manage trusted certificates

tools.subtitle: SSL checker, decoders, converters and key matcher
tools.sslCheckerDesc: Check SSL certificate of a remote server
tools.converterDesc: Convert between PEM, DER, PKCS12, PKCS7
tools.keyMatcherDesc: Verify if key, certificate and CSR match
tools.supportsFormats: Supports: PEM, DER, CRT, CER, P12/PFX, P7B, KEY, CSR
tools.privateKeyFile: Private Key File (optional if included in input)
tools.pleaseProvideItem: Please provide at least one item to match
tools.pleaseUploadOrPaste: Please upload a file or paste content to convert
tools.privateKeyRequired: Private key is required for PKCS12 conversion
tools.helpDescription: A collection of SSL/TLS tools to help you troubleshoot, verify, decode and convert certificates.
tools.helpSslChecker: Enter a hostname to check its SSL certificate. Shows validity, expiry, cipher suite, TLS version, and any issues like mismatched hostnames or expired certificates.
tools.helpCsrDecoder: Paste a CSR (Certificate Signing Request) in PEM format to see its contents including subject, public key type/size, and any requested extensions like SANs.
tools.helpCertDecoder: Paste a certificate in PEM format to see all details: subject, issuer, validity dates, extensions, key usage, fingerprints, and more.
tools.helpKeyMatcher: Verify that a private key, certificate, and/or CSR belong together by comparing their public keys. Useful before installing certificates.
tools.helpConverter: Convert certificates and keys between formats: PEM ↔ DER, PEM → PKCS12 (requires key), PEM → PKCS7. Download the converted file directly.
tools.pkcs12PasswordRequired: Password is required for PKCS12/PFX files
tools.pkcs12OutputPasswordRequired: Password is required for PKCS12 output

settings.smtpNoAuthHint: No authentication — suitable for local relay servers (Postfix, sendmail, etc.)

settings.createBackupDesc: Create a backup containing all data (database, certificates, CAs, users, configuration). The backup will be encrypted with your password.
settings.databaseResetDesc: Reset the database to initial state. This will DELETE ALL certificates, CAs, users and settings.
settings.encryptionPasswordHelper: Use a strong password - you will need it to restore the backup

settings.featureComingSoon: Feature coming soon: Upload custom HTTPS certificate

settings.restoreFromBackupDesc: Upload a .ucmbkp file to restore all data
settings.restoreWarning: This will REPLACE ALL current data with the backup contents. This action is irreversible.
settings.encryptionDesc: Encrypt all private keys stored in the database using AES-256. Keys are protected by a master key stored at /etc/ucm/master.key.
settings.encryptionEnabled: Encryption enabled successfully. All private keys are now encrypted.
settings.encryptionDisabled: Encryption disabled. Private keys are now stored unencrypted.
settings.encryptionWarningDesc: Enabling encryption will generate a master key file at /etc/ucm/master.key. Without this file, all encrypted private keys are permanently lost and cannot be recovered.
settings.encryptionCheckKeyFile: I understand the encryption key will be stored at /etc/ucm/master.key and I will back it up immediately
settings.encryptionCheckBackup: I have a recent backup of my UCM data
settings.encryptionCheckLostKeys: I understand that without this key file, all private keys are permanently lost — requiring a full reinstall and backup restore
settings.disableEncryptionConfirm: This will decrypt all private keys in the database. They will be stored unencrypted. Are you sure?
settings.noAnomaliesDesc: No suspicious activity in the last 24 hours

sso.roleMappingHelp: Map external groups or roles to UCM roles. Users in matched groups will be assigned the corresponding UCM role.

est.title: EST Protocol
est.enableEstDesc: Allow certificate enrollment via EST (RFC 7030) protocol
est.validityDaysHelp: Default validity period for EST-enrolled certificates
est.basicAuthDesc: Credentials for EST client authentication (alternative to mTLS)
est.endpointsDesc: Use these URLs in your EST client configuration
est.simpleenrollDesc: Enroll a new certificate (send PKCS#10 CSR)
est.serverkeygenDesc: Server generates key pair and certificate
est.estDisabledDesc: Enable EST in settings to allow enrollment
est.aboutEstDesc: Enrollment over Secure Transport (EST, RFC 7030) provides certificate enrollment and management using TLS and PKCS#10. It supports both mTLS and HTTP Basic Auth for client authentication.

discovery.aboutDiscoveryDesc: Scan your network to find TLS certificates deployed on servers and endpoints. Match them against your managed PKI inventory.
discovery.deleteAllConfirm: Delete all discovered certificates? This cannot be undone.
discovery.deleteDiscoveredConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this discovered certificate?
discovery.deleteProfileConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this scan profile? This will not delete discovered certificates.
discovery.errorHintDns: DNS resolution failed. The hostname could not be resolved. Check that the domain name is correct and DNS is working.
discovery.errorHintNoRoute: No route to host. The target IP is unreachable from this network. Check network connectivity and routing.
discovery.errorHintRefused: Connection was refused. The port may not be open, or the service is not running on this port.
discovery.errorHintReset: Connection was reset by the remote host. The service may have crashed, or a firewall/IDS terminated the connection.
discovery.errorHintSni: This server requires a specific hostname in the TLS handshake (SNI). The hostname provided was not recognized. Check DNS or try scanning with the correct domain name.
discovery.errorHintTimeout: Connection timed out. The host may be unreachable, a firewall may be blocking the port, or the service is very slow to respond.
discovery.noDiscoveredDescription: Run a scan to start discovering certificates on your network.
discovery.noHistoryDesc: Run a scan to see results here.
discovery.noHistoryDescription: Scan history will appear here after running your first scan.
discovery.noProfilesDesc: Create a scan profile to automate certificate discovery.
discovery.noProfilesDescription: Create a scan profile to save target configurations and enable scheduled scanning.
discovery.noResultsDesc: Run a quick scan or create a profile to discover certificates on your network.
discovery.noResultsDescription: Run a scan to discover TLS certificates on your network.
discovery.notifyEmailHelper: Uses SMTP settings if not specified
discovery.notifyOnChange: Notify when a certificate changes on an endpoint
discovery.notifyOnExpiry: Notify when discovered certificates are expiring
discovery.notifyOnNew: Notify when new certificates are discovered
discovery.portsHelp: Comma-separated list of TCP ports to scan (default: 443).
discovery.portsHelpDetailed: Comma-separated TCP ports to scan on each target (e.g., 443, 8443, 636)
discovery.profileDescPlaceholder: Optional description for this scan profile
discovery.quickScanHelp: Enter hostnames, IPs, or CIDR ranges to scan immediately without saving a profile.
discovery.selectItem: Select an item to view details
discovery.subtitle: Scan your network to discover TLS certificates
discovery.targetsHelp: One hostname or IP per line (e.g., server.example.com, 10.0.0.1:8443)
discovery.targetsHelpDetailed: One per line: hostname, IP, CIDR subnet (192.168.1.0/24), or host:port (10.0.0.1:8443)
discovery.targetsTagHelp: Type a host, IP, or CIDR and press Enter to add
discovery.title: Certificate Discovery
discovery.helpScanProfiles: Scan profiles let you save target configurations for recurring scans. Set targets, ports, and worker count, then run on demand or schedule.
discovery.helpFilters: Use the status filter pills to narrow results: Managed (known to UCM), Unmanaged (not in inventory), Error (scan failures), Expired, or Expiring Soon.
discovery.helpErrors: Scan errors include connection refused, DNS failures, TLS handshake issues, and timeouts. Check the error column for hints and retry individual targets.
discovery.helpExport: Export discovered certificates as CSV or JSON. Apply filters first to export only the subset you need.
discovery.helpSecurity: Discovery blocks internal/loopback addresses for SSRF protection. Targets are validated before scanning. All scan actions are audit-logged.

msca.title: Microsoft AD CS
msca.subtitle: Manage Microsoft Active Directory Certificate Services connections
msca.helpDescription:
Connect to Microsoft AD CS to sign certificates using your Windows Certificate Authority. Three authentication methods available:

- **Client Certificate (recommended)** — Most secure and standalone. No domain join required. Generate a client cert on your MS CA, export as PFX, then upload the cert and key here.
- **Basic Auth** — Simple username/password over HTTPS. Works without domain join. Ensure the certsrv web enrollment is configured to accept basic authentication in IIS.
- **Kerberos (optional)** — Requires the requests-kerberos pip package and the machine to be joined to the AD domain (or a keytab configured). Only needed if other auth methods are disabled on the CA.
msca.templatesFetchHint: Test the connection to auto-load available templates

warnings.authenticated_but_no_templates: Authentication OK but no templates available. Check account permissions on the CA.
warnings.authenticated_but_template_access_denied: Authentication OK but permission denied when listing templates. The account may lack enrollment permissions.

msca.noConnectionsDesc: Add a connection to sign certificates with Microsoft AD CS
msca.deleteWarning: This will remove the connection and all associated settings.
msca.pendingMessage: Certificate request is pending manager approval on Microsoft CA
msca.kerberosOptionalNote: Kerberos requires the requests-kerberos package (pip install requests-kerberos) and the machine must be joined to the Active Directory domain or have a Kerberos keytab configured.

certInput.dropOrClick: Drop a file or click to browse
certInput.parseError: Could not parse certificate from file
certInput.noCertsWithKey: No certificates with private key found
